payload.notes: Phase 3 is corrected and PR #48 premerge is green at 9ad31ce. Implement only the separately versioned bridge; preserve V1 core six-command and no-V0 dependency boundaries.
payload.summary: Implement Repository Harness V1 Phase 4 isolated V0 bridge

payload.notes: Phase 4 child of US-105. Requires immutable schema 1..=13 capture, neutral export, write-once archive, journal-bounded apply/resume/rollback, complete kill points, and proof that bridge code never enters the V1 core artifact.
payload.title: V1 isolated V0 bridge

payload.evidence: Decision 0014 supersedes automatic conversion. Local Phase 4 candidate now freezes V0, publishes exact append-only archive/export evidence under authenticated .harness-v0-archive custody, then uses normal six-command SQLite-free core install plus Phase 3 recovery to record a write-once digest receipt. Bridge grammar is exactly inspect/export/archive/version; no bridge target mutation, conversion journal, row mapping, resume, rollback, or harness-v1.db. Phase 1-4 verifiers pass locally; independent acceptance, Phase 5, Phase 7 production/platform promotion, production keys, publishing, and Phase 8 remain closed.

Independent acceptance correction remains in_progress: core now independently authenticates bridge-compatible custody.json/custody.key root identity, owner/mode/length, key digest, and HMAC before receipt preview or recovery; capture values and bridge release 1.0.0 are closed contracts; repository mode is brownfield-v1; accepted Phase 1 fixture bytes are restored to 9ad31ce and cryptographically baseline-checked while Phase 4 inventory remains separate. Re-review is still required; Phase 5, Phase 7, production promotion, publishing, and production keys remain closed.

Correction loop 2 remains in_progress: the core pins one owner-matched 0700 custody directory device/inode across key, marker, archive manifest, and payload reads, rechecks it at completion, and stores a domain-separated custody identity digest so commit, recovery, status, and audit reject rename/replacement. Production-path swap and recovery revalidation tests pass locally. Phase 1 keeps 94 accepted evidence artifacts byte-exact within the same 96-file tree; README.md and generate.py now agree that regeneration is historical-only and --check exits controlled toward the cryptographic verifier without traceback. Independent re-review, Phase 5, Phase 7, production promotion, publishing, and production keys remain closed.
